mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5. It simplifies the deployment and management of AI models in Kubernetes environments, making it ideal for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation and understanding of Kubernetes for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add Ollama Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Ollama Helm repository to your local Helm client, allowing you to install Ollama using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/helm

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Ollama Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Ollama CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Ollama to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://ollama.com/crds/ollama-crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Ollama using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama platform in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --namespace ollama --create-namespace --version 0.6.0

```

> Replace '0.6.0' with the desired version.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example custom resource to deploy a model on Ollama.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.io/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

```

> This command creates a model resource for Gemma 3.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.io/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Model Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the deployed model to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get models -n ollama

```

> This command lists all models in the Ollama namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all models and configurations, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

kubectl delete namespace ollama

```

> Uninstalls the Ollama release.
> Deletes the Ollama namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades the Ollama installation to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get models -n ollama -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --namespace ollama --version 0.6.0

kubectl get models -n ollama

```

> Backup existing models.
> Replace '0.6.0' with the desired version.
> Verify that the models are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n ollama -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model does not exist in the Ollama deployment.

**Fix:**
Ensure the model name is correct and that it has been deployed successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Ollama pods using 'kubectl get pods -n ollama' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Ollama pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available in your Kubernetes cluster or adjust the resource requests in the Ollama configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'timeout waiting for condition'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model is taking too long to become ready.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the model pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ollama' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and enhanced REST API capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old model deployment methods in favor of the new CRD approach.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.5, ensure to migrate existing models to the new CRD format.

resolution.summary: You should see the model listed with 'kubectl get models -n ollama' and its status should be 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ollama.io/v1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: gemma3
spec:
  model: gemma3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
